memory.summary: I could just relax now and ignore the looming project deadline, but my teammates might get annoyed if I slack off again. Maybe I'll do that quick chore first so I feel good about myself before hanging out.

desires: Get that quick chore done immediately so I can feel productive and justify hanging out with the group later
Avoid any awkward confrontation with my teammates about the looming project deadline by hoping they won't notice yet
Stay up late to chat with cohort_3 rather than sleeping, because being awake feels more connected than resting

goals.description: Finish the quick_chore immediately to get that instant dopamine hit and feel 'productive' before I slip into a long chat with the group.
goals.rationale: My impulsive nature craves immediate rewards over future gains. Doing this small task now justifies my decision to avoid the hard work on the term project later, allowing me to maintain the illusion of diligence while prioritizing my social connection.

goals.description: Initiate a casual hangout session with cohort_3 for the rest of the afternoon, carefully steering clear of any mention of the looming project deadline.
goals.rationale: Relationships are my highest value (0.5). Being awake with them feels more connected than sleeping. I will actively avoid discussing the project to prevent friction or guilt, hoping they won't notice how little progress we've actually made.

agent.last_reasoning: Doing the quick chore gives instant dopamine, justifying my procrastination on the term project so I can feel 'productive' before hanging out with cohort_3. It's low effort, won't drain my energy too much, and lets me slip into leisure guilt-free.
